items.q: How do you say 'I live in a house'?
items.why: Students forget that 'in' takes the dative for location; 'in einem Haus', not 'ein Haus'.
Students get the gender/case wrong; 'der Zug' is dative 'dem Zug' after 'mit'.
Students guess wrongly; it is a budget place to stay, useful holiday vocabulary.

items.q: How do you say 'I live in a house in the country'?
items.correct: Ich wohne in einem Haus auf dem Land.
items.misconception: Ich wohne in ein Haus auf dem Land.
items.why: Correct answer: Ich wohne in einem Haus auf dem Land.. “Ich wohne in ein Haus auf dem Land.” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
Correct answer: Accommodation (youth hostel). “Restaurant” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
It's Abroad. “At home” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
